name: Bhagavad Gita
description: The Song of God — a 700-verse Hindu scripture that is part of the Mahabharata

chapters.name: Arjuna Vishada Yoga
chapters.summary: Arjuna sees his relatives and teachers on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral dilemma.

chapters.name: Sankhya Yoga
chapters.summary: Krishna teaches about the immortality of the soul, the nature of duty, and the path of wisdom.

chapters.name: Karma Yoga
chapters.summary: Krishna explains the importance of selfless action and performing one's duty without attachment to results.

chapters.name: Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga
chapters.nameMeaning: The Yoga of Knowledge and Renunciation of Action
chapters.summary: Krishna reveals the divine nature of his incarnations and the importance of knowledge combined with action.

chapters.name: Karma Sanyasa Yoga
chapters.summary: Krishna compares renunciation of action with selfless action, showing both lead to liberation.

chapters.name: Dhyana Yoga
chapters.summary: Krishna teaches the practice of meditation, self-control and the state of inner peace.

chapters.name: Jnana Vijnana Yoga
chapters.nameMeaning: The Yoga of Knowledge and Wisdom
chapters.summary: Krishna reveals his divine nature and explains the difference between material and spiritual knowledge.

chapters.name: Aksara Brahma Yoga
chapters.nameMeaning: The Yoga of the Imperishable Absolute
chapters.summary: Krishna explains the nature of the supreme, the process of death, and how to attain the eternal.

chapters.name: Raja Vidya Raja Guhya Yoga
chapters.nameMeaning: The Yoga of Royal Knowledge and Royal Secret
chapters.summary: Krishna shares the most confidential knowledge about devotion and his relationship with all beings.

chapters.name: Vibhuti Yoga
chapters.summary: Krishna describes his divine manifestations in the world to inspire devotion.

chapters.name: Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga
chapters.nameMeaning: The Yoga of the Vision of the Universal Form
chapters.summary: Arjuna is granted divine vision to see Krishna's cosmic universal form.

chapters.name: Bhakti Yoga
chapters.summary: Krishna describes the path of devotion and qualities of a true devotee.

chapters.name: Kshetra Kshetragna Vibhaga Yoga
chapters.nameMeaning: The Yoga of the Field and the Knower
chapters.summary: Krishna explains the body (field) and the soul (knower of the field) and the difference between them.

chapters.name: Gunatraya Vibhaga Yoga
chapters.nameMeaning: The Yoga of the Three Gunas
chapters.summary: Krishna describes the three qualities of nature — sattva, rajas, and tamas — and how they bind the soul.

chapters.name: Purushottama Yoga
chapters.nameMeaning: The Yoga of the Supreme Person
chapters.summary: Krishna describes the eternal tree of existence and reveals himself as the Supreme Person.

chapters.name: Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yoga
chapters.nameMeaning: The Yoga of Divine and Demonic Qualities
chapters.summary: Krishna describes divine and demonic qualities and encourages cultivating the divine nature.

chapters.name: Shraddhatraya Vibhaga Yoga
chapters.nameMeaning: The Yoga of the Three Divisions of Faith
chapters.summary: Krishna explains the three types of faith, food, sacrifice, austerity, and charity.

chapters.name: Moksha Sanyasa Yoga
chapters.nameMeaning: The Yoga of Liberation through Renunciation
chapters.summary: The final teaching — Krishna summarizes all paths and urges Arjuna to surrender to the divine will.
